name: Sanctuary of Oro Loop from Murgia
name_original: Santuario del Oro — circular desde Murgia por el Valle de Zuia
description: Cycle a moderate 15.4-mile loop from Murgia to Santuario del Oro in Valle de Zuia, enjoying challenging climbs and panoramic views.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Zuya/Zuia, located in the Gorbea Foothills of Álava, offers varied landscapes for cyclists. The region is characterized by green, hilly terrain, providing options for both leisurely rides and more challenging routes with elevation gains. An extensive network of cycle paths, known as "bidegorris," and greenways ensures dedicated and scenic routes. Features like the Ullibarri-Gamboa Reservoir and regional forests contribute to the diverse cycling environment. Best touring cycling routes around Zuya/Zuia The most popular touring cycling route is Sanctuary of Oro Loop from Murgia, a 15.4 miles (24.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 41 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the Sanctuary of Oro amidst the hilly landscape. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Drinking water fountain – Zárate loop from Murgia, a difficult 30.5 miles (49.1 km) path. This longer route provides a challenging ride through the local terrain. Local touring cyclists also love the Gorbea Summit – Gorbea Summit loop from Murgia, a 16.2 miles (26.1 km) trail leading through the Gorbea Foothills, often completed in about 3 hours 15 minutes. Touring cycling around Zuya/Zuia is defined by green, hilly landscapes, extensive cycling networks, and routes passing through forests and near the Ullibarri-Gamboa Reservoir. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Zuya/Zuia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 9 reviews. More than 270 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Zuya/Zuia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Zuya/Zuia?
A: There are over 15 touring cycling routes around Zuya/Zuia, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. The region features a mix of terrains, from leisurely rides to more challenging routes with significant elevation gains.

Q: What kind of terrain and difficulty can I expect on touring cycling routes around Zuya/Zuia?
A: Zuya/Zuia, situated in the Gorbea Foothills, offers green, hilly landscapes. You'll find a mix of routes, with 8 classified as moderate and 7 as difficult, providing options for both relaxed rides and more adventurous challenges. The area also boasts an extensive network of cycle paths, known as "bidegorris," and greenways for safe and scenic cycling.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Zuya/Zuia?
A: Yes, Zuya/Zuia offers routes suitable for families. While specific family-friendly routes are not detailed in the top selections, the region's extensive network of "bidegorris" and greenways often provides safer, flatter sections ideal for family outings. Look for routes with lower elevation gains and shorter distances for a more relaxed experience.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the touring cycling trails in Zuya/Zuia?
A: Generally, many outdoor trails in the Basque Country are dog-friendly, especially if your dog is well-behaved and kept on a leash. However, specific regulations can vary by protected area or local municipality. It's always best to check local signage or official tourism information for any particular route or natural park you plan to visit to ensure compliance.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or natural attractions I can see along the cycling routes?
A: Cycling routes in Zuya/Zuia often pass through picturesque scenery and near significant natural and cultural landmarks. You can explore areas near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2456239" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Our Lady of Oro&lt;/a&gt;, or experience the lushness of the Gorbea Foothills. Some routes also lead towards the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4136536" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorbea Summit&lt;/a&gt; or past the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3009759" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Uguzpe Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The Ullibarri-Gamboa Reservoir is also a prominent natural feature integrated into several cycling routes in the wider area.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Zuya/Zuia?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in Zuya/Zuia are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1068575605/sanctuary-of-oro-loop-from-murgia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Oro Loop from Murgia&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15044103" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Drinking water fountain – Zárate loop from Murgia&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1289331" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorbea Summit – Gorbea Summit loop from Murgia&lt;/a&gt;. These circular routes make planning your ride convenient.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Zuya/Zuia?
A: The temperate climate and oceanic influences in Zuya/Zuia allow for cycling in all seasons. However, for the most comfortable experience, spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October) generally offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summers can be warm, while winters are mild but may bring more rain.

Q: Where can I find parking for cyclists near the touring routes in Zuya/Zuia?
A: Parking is generally available in and around the main towns like Murgia, which serves as a common starting point for many routes. For specific trailheads, look for designated parking areas. The Ullibarri-Gamboa Reservoir area, for instance, has provincial parks like Garaio and Landa that offer access and parking facilities.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by a waterfall?
A: Yes, one notable route, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31878738" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fountain in Orduña – Gújuli Waterfall loop from Murgia&lt;/a&gt;, specifically includes a waterfall. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3009759" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Uguzpe Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a highlight in the region that can be incorporated into or viewed from certain routes.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Zuya/Zuia?
A: The touring cycling routes in Zuya/Zuia are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.9 stars from over 9 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the extensive network of cycle paths, and the picturesque green, hilly landscapes that offer both scenic beauty and engaging gradients.

Q: Can I access Zuya/Zuia's cycling routes using public transport?
A: While Zuia is accessible by public transport, especially from larger cities in Álava like Vitoria-Gasteiz, direct access to specific trailheads might require some planning. Buses typically serve the main towns like Murgia. It's advisable to check local bus schedules and policies regarding bicycle transport before your trip.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Zuya/Zuia offers several challenging routes for experienced touring cyclists. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32046524" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zárate – Echevarría Bakery loop from Zuia&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 39.5 km route with significant elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31878738" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fountain in Orduña – Gújuli Waterfall loop from Murgia&lt;/a&gt; is another difficult option, spanning over 55 km with substantial climbing.
tips: Beautiful Romanesque-style church built in the 12th century. It stands out for its impressive façade decorated with sculptures and for its square tower. Inside you can find wall paintings from the 13th and 14th centuries, as well as a Baroque altarpiece from the 18th century. The church of San Martín is an important historical and artistic monument in the area.

The Sanctuary of Our Lady of Gold has been a place of pilgrimage for centuries, and its origin dates back to the Middle Ages. The current church was built in the 16th century and has an 18th century Baroque altarpiece. The sanctuary is surrounded by a privileged natural environment, which invites tranquility and contemplation.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is designed for touring bicycles and typically takes around 1 hour and 41 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your cycling pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in Murgia, which serves as the starting point for this circular route. Specific parking areas may vary, but Murgia is a well-known access point for the Valle de Zuia.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as 'moderate' in difficulty. It features significant elevation changes, including challenging climbs, especially near the Sanctuary of Our Lady of Oro. While not extreme, it's best suited for cyclists with some experience looking for a good workout rather than absolute beginners.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain combines paved roads with significant climbs, particularly as you ascend towards the Sanctuary of Our Lady of Oro, which sits at approximately 850 meters above sea level. You can expect good climbs with tight stretches, offering a rewarding challenge for touring cyclists. The route also passes through tranquil forests and offers panoramic views.

faqs.question: What notable sights or viewpoints will I encounter?
faqs.answer: The route offers stunning panoramic views of the Valle de Zuia and the Llanada Alavesa from the elevated position of the Sanctuary of Our Lady of Oro. You'll also pass by the historic Church of San Martín (Jugo), adding a cultural dimension to your ride. The natural environment, with its beech and oak forests, is a highlight in itself.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Autumn is particularly recommended for cycling this route. The landscape becomes 'indescribable' as the leaves change to vibrant reddish hues, offering a truly spectacular experience. Spring and early summer also provide pleasant conditions with lush greenery.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route is primarily for touring bicycles, dogs are generally welcome in the Gorbeia Natural Park area, provided they are kept on a leash, especially to protect local wildlife and livestock. Always ensure your dog is comfortable with the distance and terrain.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required for cycling in Gorbeia Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on established routes within Gorbeia Natural Park. However, always respect park regulations, stay on marked trails, and practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several official trails. You will find yourself on parts of the CIMA AL12 Santuario del Oro, Baia ibaia La Encontradan, Senda Murgia-Jugo igoera, Senda Jugatxi-Murgia igoera, and Camino de Aperregi a Ganalto, among others.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: Murgia, as the starting and ending point, offers amenities like cafes and pubs. While the route itself passes through natural areas, it's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially for the climbs. Check local listings for establishments in Murgia before you set off.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this touring bicycle trip?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and elevation changes, pack plenty of water, energy snacks, and appropriate cycling gear. A helmet is essential. Consider bringing layers of clothing, as temperatures can vary, especially at higher elevations. A basic repair kit and a fully charged phone are also recommended.

waypoints.name: Sanctuary of Our Lady of Oro
